political.s: Block Inc contributed $503,145 in the 2024 election cycle and spent $2,120,000 on federal lobbying. The company maintains a balanced approach to political engagement across both parties, with active participation in regulatory matters affecting fintech and payments.

charity.s: Block pledged $100 million toward minority and underserved communities, including $25 million to The Keepers Fund and $25 million to The Black Economic Development Fund. The company facilitated $850+ million in PPP loans through Square Capital and continues community support initiatives.
charity.focus: Financial inclusion, minority-owned businesses, underserved communities

environment.s: Block committed to net zero carbon operations by 2030 and reported completing SBTi verification of near-term emission reduction targets. In 2023, the company funded removal of 1.5 million pounds of oceanbound plastics and conducted global carbon and plastic audits across all business units.

labor.s: In February 2026, Block announced layoffs of over 4,000 employees (40% of workforce) citing AI efficiency. Prior reports indicated compliance lapses and whistleblower allegations of management ignoring anti-money laundering rule breaches. DEI ratings on Glassdoor average 4.1/5, equal to IT sector average.

dei.s: Block maintains public commitment to diversity and inclusion with quarterly reporting to the Board on I&D metrics. Shareholders filed resolutions requesting disclosure of EEO-1 forms and quantitative DEI data by gender, race, and ethnicity. The company reports 41% women and 21% underrepresented minorities as of 2020.

privacy.s: Block's Cash App and Square platforms faced multiple federal investigations starting in 2023 for inadequate due diligence, identity verification, and reporting of suspicious transactions. Whistleblower allegations indicated management ignored compliance lapses related to sanctioned nations and terrorist financing.

execPay.s: Block's highest-paid executive, Sarah Friar, earned $3.88 million. The average Block executive compensation is $227,558 annually. Available public data does not clearly disclose CEO-to-median-employee pay ratio in recent filings.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
